en_01_date_number.wav.text: Let's meet on March 3rd at 4 o'clock. I already sent you the 12 invoices we talked about.
en_01_date_number.wav.note: two conversational sentences with a date and a number

en_02_name_email.wav.text: John Anderson said to reach him at john dot anderson at example dot com if you have questions.
en_02_name_email.wav.note: proper name and an email-style phrase

en_03_rambling.wav.text: So, um, I was thinking maybe we should, you know, push the meeting back a little, because honestly I haven't finished the report yet, and, uh, my internet was down half the morning, which was really frustrating, so yeah, let's just say Thursday instead, if that works for you.
en_03_rambling.wav.note: longer ~15s rambling passage with filler words and a self-correction-like restart

bg_01_date_number.wav.text: Ще се видим на трети март в четири часа. Вече ти изпратих дванайсетте фактури, за които говорихме.
bg_01_date_number.wav.note: two conversational sentences with число (number) and дата (date)

bg_02_name_address.wav.text: Иван Петров каза да му пишете на улица Витоша номер седем, ако имате въпроси.

bg_03_rambling.wav.text: Ами, значи, мислех си, че може би трябва да отложим срещата малко, защото честно казано още не съм готов с доклада, а и интернетът ми не работеше половината сутрин, което беше доста дразнещо, така че хайде просто да кажем четвъртък, ако си свободен през юни.
bg_03_rambling.wav.note: longer ~15s passage with natural Cyrillic including ъ, щ, ю, я

silence_2s.wav.note: 2 seconds of 16kHz mono silence, generated via Python wave module, for the hallucination check
